trials.reason: Book B groups the entire live-derivation infrastructure (kernel wiring, association helpers, headword expressions, and their tests) into one coherent section before moving to consumers of it, whereas Book A scatters the same EntryQueryHelpers.cs pieces across many separate sections in a less logical build-up.

Book A groups related chunks into 7 coherent sections (core helpers, kernel wiring, tests, snapshots) so each builds on prior context, whereas Book B splinters the same file into 22 fragmented sections that scatter closely related methods and interleave test/implementation chunks without a clear cumulative build-up.

Book B groups the entire EntryQueryHelpers/LcmCrdtKernel infrastructure build-up (associations, morph type, headword expressions, sqlite helpers) into fewer, cohesive sections before moving to the consumer-side FwData/MiniLcm test changes, whereas Book A splits EntryQueryHelpers edits and their tests across scattered, interleaved chunks that jump between infrastructure and consumers repeatedly.

metadata.note: single-family judge (Claude): self-preference caveat applies; generator/judge ids must differ where possible
